title: Variables
description: Declare and initialize values using var and short declaration.

concept.definition: Go variables store typed values and can be declared with var or :=.
concept.analogy: A variable is a labeled slot that holds one value type.
keyPoints: Zero values exist for uninitialized vars

defaultCode:
package main

import "fmt"

func main() {
	name := "StepViz"
	count := 1
	count++
	fmt.Println(name, count)
}

title: Core Data Types
description: Use built-in numeric, string, and boolean types.

concept.definition: Go includes strong static types like int, float64, string, and bool.
concept.analogy: Types are strict containers for valid values.
keyPoints: string for text and bool for true/false

defaultCode:
package main

import "fmt"

func main() {
	age := 20
	score := 9.5
	name := "Ari"
	active := true
	fmt.Println(age, score, name, active)
}

title: Functions
description: Write reusable functions with explicit parameter and return types.

concept.definition: Functions encapsulate logic and define input/output contracts.
concept.analogy: A function is a machine that transforms input into output.

title: Control Flow
description: Use if/else and switch for branching logic.

concept.definition: Control flow chooses which branch executes based on conditions.
concept.analogy: Branches are decision gates in program flow.

title: Loops
description: Repeat operations with Go's for loop forms.

concept.definition: Go uses for as its single loop keyword for many iteration styles.
concept.analogy: A loop is a repeat lane for a block of code.

title: Arrays & Slices
description: Store ordered collections with fixed and dynamic sizing.

concept.definition: Arrays have fixed length; slices are flexible views over arrays.
concept.analogy: Array is a fixed tray, slice is a flexible window on items.
keyPoints: Array length is part of type

title: Maps
description: Use key-value mappings for fast lookup.

concept.definition: Maps store key-value pairs with efficient key-based access.
concept.analogy: A map is a dictionary table for quick retrieval.
keyPoints: Create with map literals or make

title: Structs & Methods
description: Model data and behavior with structs and receiver methods.

concept.definition: Structs group related fields; methods attach behavior to types.
concept.analogy: Struct is a blueprint, methods are its actions.

title: Interfaces
description: Define behavior contracts with method sets.

concept.definition: Interfaces describe behavior and are satisfied implicitly.
concept.analogy: An interface is a capability checklist.

title: Error Handling
description: Handle failures explicitly using error values.

concept.definition: Go returns errors as values instead of exceptions.
concept.analogy: Error handling is a checked return lane after each risky call.
keyPoints: if err != nil is standard pattern

title: Packages
description: Organize and reuse code through package imports.

concept.definition: Packages are the unit of modularity in Go projects.
concept.analogy: Packages are toolboxes with named public tools.

title: Goroutines & Channels
description: Write concurrent code with goroutines and channel communication.

concept.definition: Goroutines run functions concurrently and channels coordinate data flow.
concept.analogy: Goroutines are workers; channels are message pipes.
